architecture.summary: KICP is a three-module pretraining framework for knowledge-based QA. Knowledge Injection (KI) converts knowledge graph triples into sentences via text characterization, sentence construction, and masking. Knowledge Adaptation (KA) freezes the base language model and trains a learnable adapter on top to retain natural language understanding while learning from synthetic KG-derived corpus. Curriculum Reasoning (CR) constructs three increasingly difficult pretraining corpora (single triples, chain-of-thought reasoning steps, final compositions) and trains the adapter via curriculum learning. After pretraining, the frozen LM + trained adapter are fine-tuned on downstream QA tasks.
trained_components: knowledge adapter (BERT-based with halved hidden dimension)

biblio.title: A Knowledge-Injected Curriculum Pretraining Framework for Question Answering

claims.statement: KICP outperforms all baselines on CN-QA with F1 score of 0.639 and EM of 0.480
KICP achieves F1 of 0.880 and EM of 0.819 on ComplexWebQuestions
KICP achieves accuracy of 0.911 on FreebaseQA
KICP achieves accuracy of 0.809 on Math23K
Knowledge adaptation (KA) alone improves CN-QA F1 to 0.633 compared to BERT baseline at 0.607
On hard questions in FreebaseQA, KICP achieves accuracy of 0.907 compared to BERT at 0.891

The curriculum pretraining with three lessons (knowledge learning, chain-of-thought learning, composition learning) reduces training difficulty and achieves lower final loss in lesson 3 compared to direct training on lesson 3
Knowledge injection does not bring much improvement and even has negative effect on Math23K, likely because Math23K requires NLU much more than knowledge
